categories.name: skills
categories.definition: Local instruction packs stored as SKILL.md files. They change how the agent should approach a task.
categories.activation_rule: Use a skill when the user names it or when the task clearly matches its description.
categories.agent_habit: Read the full SKILL.md before task work, then read only required references.

categories.name: plugins
categories.definition: Local bundles that can contribute skills, MCP servers, apps, and other tools.
categories.activation_rule: Do not invoke a plugin directly. Use the skills and tools that the plugin exposes.
categories.agent_habit: Prefer official upstream packs when the user asks for a known plugin or skill pack.

categories.name: mcp_tools
categories.definition: Callable functions exposed by MCP servers or apps.
categories.activation_rule: Use only MCP tools available in the current active tool list or discovered through tool_search.
categories.agent_habit: Validate the tool schema before calling. Do not invent server names or parameters.

categories.name: mcp_resources
categories.definition: Read-only context objects shared by MCP servers.
categories.activation_rule: Prefer resources over web search when the relevant server provides the needed data.
categories.agent_habit: List or read resources only when they are relevant to the current task.

categories.name: apps_connectors
categories.definition: Installed user-data or external-service connectors exposed as MCP tools.
categories.activation_rule: Use when the user explicitly asks for that app data or when the context clearly implies it.
categories.agent_habit: Use tool_search for deferred tools. Ask for installs only after exact-match discovery.

categories.name: local_shell_tools
categories.definition: Runtime tools for file inspection, commands, patching, plans, image viewing, and local verification.
categories.activation_rule: Use for repo work, local tests, builds, and file edits.
categories.agent_habit: Use rg for search, multi_tool_use.parallel for independent reads, and apply_patch for manual edits.

categories.name: web_and_browser_tools
categories.definition: Tools for current facts, external docs, source verification, screenshots, and browser checks.
categories.activation_rule: Use when the user asks to browse or when information may have changed.
categories.agent_habit: Cite sources and prefer official docs for product, API, legal, medical, financial, or software claims.

categories.name: memory
categories.definition: Local notes from prior runs that can preserve project preferences and repeated failure modes.
categories.activation_rule: Use by default for non-trivial workspace tasks that may depend on prior decisions.
categories.agent_habit: Treat memory as a lead. Verify drift-prone facts when cheap, and append the required memory citation block when used.

negative_rules: Do not train on hidden reasoning, secrets, private tokens, or personal messages.
Do not claim a tool exists because it appeared in an old memory entry.
Do not request plugin installation for adjacent capabilities the user did not ask for.
Do not call MCP tools with guessed fields.
Do not edit upstream trainer internals for Codex app environment data.
